zh.lifeSummary: 當Margaret de Ros於大約1429年出生在英國萊希斯特斯爾貝爾沃易 卡素時，她的父親Sir Thomas de Ros25歲，母親Eleanor Beauchamp22歲。她於大約1460年與Sir Thomas Burgh 1st Baron Burgh of Gainsborough結婚。他們至少是3個兒子和3個女兒的父母。她去世於1488年12月10日在英國林可爾史爾蓋斯波洛享年60歲，埋葬在英國林可爾史爾蓋斯波洛。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Margaret de Ros于大约1429年出生在英国萊希斯特斯爾貝爾沃易 卡素时，她的父亲Sir Thomas de Ros25岁，母亲Eleanor Beauchamp22岁。她于大约1460年与Sir Thomas Burgh 1st Baron Burgh of Gainsborough结婚。他们至少是3个儿子和3个女儿的父母。她去世于1488年12月10日在英国林可爾史爾蓋斯波洛享年60岁，埋葬在英国林可爾史爾蓋斯波洛。

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Ros
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Enrique, Luis, Jose, Alberto, Eduardo, Rafael, Elena, Fidel, Joaquin, Manuel, Pablo, Ramon.  Southeast Asian Leang, Phon, Lap, Phan, Pho, Saroeun, Sophal, Sophan, Sophea, Tha, Thang, Yen.
Catalan: nickname for someone with blond hair or a fair complexion, ultimately from Latin russus.

variants.name: Rus
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Romanian Simion, Grigore, Doina, Dorel, Ilie, Liviu, Petru, Vasile.

Czech, Slovak, and Romanian (of Slavic origin): ethnic name for a Russian, ultimately derived from an Old Norse term meaning ‘oarsman, rower’ (from the root roths-), a reference to the fact that the Russian state was established by Scandinavians who rowed their ships up rivers inland from the Baltic sea. In some cases this may also be origin of the Slovenian and Croatian surname (see 3 below).
Czech: descriptive nickname from the adjective rusý ‘blond’.

variants.name: Ross
meanings: Scottish: habitational name from one or other of a number of Scottish and English places called Ross or Roos(e), especially Roose (Lancashire) and Roos (East Yorkshire). The placenames derive from a British ancestor of Welsh rhos ‘moor, heath, plain’, which is the sense of the cognate Gaelic word ros. The Scottish surname has at least three origins. First, the Anglo-Norman family from Roos (East Yorkshire) was introduced to Scotland when Robert of Roos, lord of Wark Castle (Northumberland), married Isabella, an illegitimate daughter of King William the Lion. Second, various families took the name from the province of Ross in northern Scotland and other places of that name. Third, there were Norman families in Scotland by the 13th century who probably derived their name from Rots in Normandy (see 2 below). The descendants of Godfrey de Ros, tenant of the de Morville lords of Cunninghame, were major landholders in Ayrshire, and almost certainly took their name from Rots. The Rose family of Kilravock (Nairnshire) may take their name from either of these three (see Rose ). The lairds of Balnagown adopted the surname Ross after the earldom of Ross (to which they considered themselves rightful heirs) had passed into other hands through the female line.

English (of Norman origin): habitational name from Rots in Calvados (France), probably named with the ancient Germanic element rod ‘clearing’ (compare Rhodes ). This was the original home of a family de Ros, who were established in Kent in 1130 and had major estates in Kent until well into the 13th century.

English: habitational name from Wrose, in Shipley, near Bradford (Yorkshire), with re-spelling of Wr- as R- due to the loss of /w/ before /r/ in early modern English pronunciation. The spelling Wrose is no longer current. The placename derives from Old English wrāse ‘knot, something twisted’, referring to the steep-sided hill on which the settlement stands, with the sense ‘broken or twisting hill’.

nameMeanings.name: Margaret
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: An extremely common given name from the Middle Ages onwards, derived via Old French Marguerite and Latin Margarita from Greek Margarītēs, from margaron ‘pearl’, a word ultimately of Hebrew origin. The name was always understood to mean ‘pearl’ throughout the Middle Ages. The first St Margaret was martyred at Antioch in Pisidia during the persecution instigated by the Emperor Diocletian in the early 4th century. However, there is some doubt about her name, as the same saint is venerated in the Orthodox Church as Marina . There were several other saintly bearers of the name, including St Margaret of Scotland ( d. 1093 ), wife of King Malcolm Canmore and daughter of Edmund Ironside of England. It was also the name of the wife of Henry VI of England, Margaret of Anjou ( 1430–82 ), and of Margaret Tudor ( 1489–1541 ), sister of Henry VIII , who married James IV of Scotland and ruled as regent there after his death. See also Margery , Marjorie .

variants.name: Margareta
meanings: From the Latin form of Greek Margarītēs. See Margaret .

variants.name: Margaretta
meanings: An extremely common given name from the Middle Ages onwards, derived via Old French Marguerite and Latin Margarita from Greek Margarītēs, from margaron ‘pearl’, a word ultimately of Hebrew origin. The name was always understood to mean ‘pearl’ throughout the Middle Ages. The first St Margaret was martyred at Antioch in Pisidia during the persecution instigated by the Emperor Diocletian in the early 4th century. However, there is some doubt about her name, as the same saint is venerated in the Orthodox Church as Marina . There were several other saintly bearers of the name, including St Margaret of Scotland ( d. 1093 ), wife of King Malcolm Canmore and daughter of Edmund Ironside of England. It was also the name of the wife of Henry VI of England, Margaret of Anjou ( 1430–82 ), and of Margaret Tudor ( 1489–1541 ), sister of Henry VIII , who married James IV of Scotland and ruled as regent there after his death. See also Margery , Marjorie .

historicEvents.title: Wars of the Roses
historicEvents.description: Wars of the Roses was a series of English civil wars for control of the throne of England between the royal families House of Lancaster and the House of York. The name of the war was derived as the red rose was the symbol of the Lancastrians and the white rose was the symbol of the Yorks.

en.lifeSummary: When Margaret de Ros was born about 1429, in Belvoir Castle, Leicestershire, England, her father, Sir Thomas de Ros, was 25 and her mother, Eleanor Beauchamp, was 22. She married Sir Thomas Burgh 1st Baron Burgh of Gainsborough about 1460. They were the parents of at least 3 sons and 3 daughters. She died on 10 December 1488, in Gainsborough, Lincolnshire, England, at the age of 60, and was buried in Gainsborough, Lincolnshire, England.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Margaret de Ros est née environ 1429, à Belvoir Castle, Leicestershire, Angleterre, son père, Sir Thomas de Ros, avait 25 ans et sa mère, Eleanor Beauchamp, avait 22 ans. Elle a épousé Sir Thomas Burgh 1st Baron Burgh of Gainsborough environ 1460. Ils ont eu au moins 3 fils et 3 filles. Elle est décédée le 10 décembre 1488, à Gainsborough, Lincolnshire, Angleterre, à l’âge de 60 ans, et a été enterrée à Gainsborough, Lincolnshire, Angleterre.

de.lifeSummary: Als Margaret de Ros um 1429 in Belvoir Castle, Leicestershire, England geboren wurde, war ihr Vater Sir Thomas de Ros 25 und ihre Mutter Eleanor Beauchamp 22. Sie hat um 1460 Sir Thomas Burgh 1st Baron Burgh of Gainsborough geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 3 Söhne und 3 Töchter. Sie starb am 10. Dezember 1488 im Alter von 60 Jahren in Gainsborough, Lincolnshire, England und wurde in Gainsborough, Lincolnshire, England begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Margaret de Ros è nata il circa 1429 nel Belvoir Castle, Leicestershire, England, sua padre, Sir Thomas de Ros, aveva 25 anni e sua madre, Eleanor Beauchamp, aveva 22 anni. Lei sposato Sir Thomas Burgh 1st Baron Burgh of Gainsborough circa 1460. Erano genitori di almeno 3 figli e 3 figlie. Morì il 10 dicembre 1488, nel Gainsborough, Lincolnshire, England, all'età di 60 anni, e fu sepolta a Gainsborough, Lincolnshire, England.

ja.lifeSummary: Margaret de Rosが約1429年にイギリスレアイセステルシュアイアベルボイル カストルで生まれた時，父Sir Thomas de Rosは25歳，母Eleanor Beauchampは22歳であった。約1460年にSir Thomas Burgh 1st Baron Burgh of Gainsboroughと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも3人の息子と3人の娘の親であった。彼女は1488年12月10日にイギリスリンコルンシュアイアガインスボロウで60歳で死去し，イギリスリンコルンシュアイアガインスボロウにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Margaret de Ros(이)가 대략 1429년 영국 레이스스터실르 벨보이르 카스틀에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Sir Thomas de Ros(은)는 25세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Eleanor Beauchamp(은)는 22세였다. 그녀는 대략 1460년 Sir Thomas Burgh 1st Baron Burgh of Gainsborough(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 세 명의 아들과 세 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1488년 12월 10일 영국 링코실르 게인스버루에서 60세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 링코실르 게인스버루에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Margaret de Ros nasceu aproximadamente 1429, em Belvoir Castle, Leicestershire, Inglaterra, seu pai, Sir Thomas de Ros, tinha 25 anos e sua mãe, Eleanor Beauchamp, tinha 22 anos. Ela casou-se com Sir Thomas Burgh 1st Baron Burgh of Gainsborough aproximadamente 1460. Eles tiveram pelo menos 3 filhos e 3 filhas. Ela faleceu em 10 de dezembro de 1488, em Gainsborough, Lincolnshire, Inglaterra, com 60 anos, e foi sepultada em Gainsborough, Lincolnshire, Inglaterra.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Margaret de Ros родилась около 1429 г. в местности белвоир кастл, лисестиршир, Англия, её отцу по имени Sir Thomas de Ros было 25 лет, а матери, которую звали Eleanor Beauchamp, 22. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Sir Thomas Burgh 1st Baron Burgh of Gainsborough около 1460 г. У них было по крайней мере 3 сына и 3 дочери. Она умерла 10 декабря 1488 г. в местности геинсбороую, линколншир, Англия в возрасте 60 лет и была похоронена в местности геинсбороую, линколншир, Англия.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Margaret de Ros nació aproximadamente 1429, en Belvoir Castle, Leicestershire, Inglaterra, su papá, Sir Thomas de Ros, tenía 25 años y su mamá, Eleanor Beauchamp, tenía 22 años. Se casó con Sir Thomas Burgh 1st Baron Burgh of Gainsborough aproximadamente 1460. Fueron padres de por lo menos 3 hijos y 3 hijas. Murió el 10 de diciembre de 1488, en Gainsborough, Lincolnshire, Inglaterra, a la edad de 60 años, y fue sepultada en Gainsborough, Lincolnshire, Inglaterra.
